Requires urgent action change addressing climate

questions.solution: Addressing climate change requires urgent action

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Eddy-current loss in a magnetic core varies with frequency f and flux density Bm as:
questions.solution: Eddy loss ∝ f²Bm²t² (lamination thickness t); hysteresis loss ∝ f·Bm^1.6 (Steinmetz).

questions.stem: Three resistors 68Ω, 47Ω, 1000Ω in series with 18V. I:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If one wattmeter in the two-wattmeter method reads zero, the load power factor is:
questions.solution: W₂ = VLIL cos(30°+φ) = 0 when 30°+φ = 90°, i.e. φ = 60°, pf = cos60° = 0.5.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Starting torque ∝ V²
questions.solution: Torque very sensitive to voltage dips. 10% V drop → 19% T drop.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Magnetic hysteresis:
options: B lags H (domain wall motion). Loop area = energy loss/cycle
questions.solution: Hysteresis loss: P_h = K_h·f·B_m^1.6. Reduced by silicon steel.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Power-factor improvement of an inductive load is usually done by connecting:
questions.solution: A shunt capacitor supplies leading reactive power, raising the lagging power factor.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the two-wattmeter method the readings are W₁ = 1200 W and W₂ = 400 W. The total 3-phase power is:
questions.solution: Total power = W₁ + W₂ = 1200 + 400 = 1600 W (the difference relates to reactive power).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A balanced 3-phase load draws 25 A at 415 V line, pf 0.9. The total power is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Why can a transformer not step up a DC voltage?
options: DC cannot flow through copper windings.
Steady DC produces no changing flux, so no EMF is induced in the secondary.
DC has too high a frequency.
The core saturates instantly for all DC.
questions.solution: Transformer action needs dΦ/dt; constant DC gives zero rate of change and hence zero secondary EMF.

questions.solution: At f>fr, XL > XC. Phase angle positive. Power factor lagging.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two resistors of 6 Ω and 12 Ω are connected in series. The equivalent resistance is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The no-load current of a transformer is small and highly reactive because:
options: It mainly supplies the magnetizing reactive power, with little core loss component
It supplies the full load power
questions.solution: No-load current is the magnetizing current plus a small loss component, so it is small and lags by nearly 90°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Proximity effect:
options: Current redistribution due to nearby conductors
questions.solution: Proximity: due to mutual inductance between adjacent conductors. Increases effective R.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The balanced Wheatstone bridge shown has P = 20 Ω, Q = 200 Ω and S = 60 Ω (galvanometer reads zero). The unknown resistance R is:
questions.solution: At balance P/Q = R/S, so R = P·S/Q = 20×60/200 = 6 Ω.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transformers are rated in kVA rather than kW because:
options: Their losses depend on voltage and current, not on the load power factor
questions.solution: Iron loss depends on voltage and copper loss on current; both are independent of the load power factor, so apparent power (kVA) is the rating.
